title: Subitem app automations
excerpt: I am currently struggling with managing various automations/integrations at the subitem level. i think this seems to generally be an area of limitation for the platform. In particular, when creating a custom automation t…

posts.author: jdfont4 (John Fontenault)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am currently struggling with managing various automations/integrations at the subitem level. i think this seems to generally be an area of limitation for the platform. In particular, when creating a custom automation that involves something like a subitem status change, the action or then condition can be a Monday action, but I would like this to be an app action. This is supported at the item level, but when trying to select the app action Monday.com states “subitems are not yet supported in apps automations”.

  1. is there a suitable workaround without having to use multiple trigger columns?
  2. is there an expected timeframe to enable this use case?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Us app devs are right there with you, we’d love if monday would enhance the custom automation builder to support apps for subitems, and allow us to even have actions that change a status for example. It is so limited for apps.

posts.author: vhana (Victoria Hana)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed - we utilize subitems at a large level for creative requests and would love to be able to integrate our Sharepoint Embed with subitems so we can add images into our Sharepoint. Kind of pointless to have to do it all by hand.

posts.author: wnm (Wolfram Müller)
posts.cookedHtml:
there is an open feature request for this, make sure to upvote

Subitem status updates are unsupported in apps automations Feature requests
I should not have to reorganize the scale of my workspace simply to be able to send email updates on subitem statuses. These two workarounds were suggested by customer support - “You would need to change the workflow of the board hierarchy where subitems are items, items become groups and groups become separate boards to support what I believe you want to do. Another option here is that if the email can wait until all subitem tasks are complete, you can have a two-automation process where one au…

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jdfont4, @anon29275264, @vhana

I’m Daniel from Fantasy Media. We developed an app called subitem automations that provides robust automation capabilities to subitems to solve for most (if not all) of the gaps in the native automations.

The app has almost 18K installs with an average rating of 4.9/5.

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - https://monday.com/marketplace/10000287

Hope that helps and if any capability is missing in order to meet your use case. Feel free to send me an email at hello@fantasymedia.io
